task_completion.visual_evidence: The Wazuh/OpenSearch Dashboards interface shows a dashboard being created or edited with the title 'Web_Attack_Analysis'.

task_completion.description: Create a metric visualization for critical web alerts
task_completion.visual_evidence: A metric visualization (a single large number) is visible. The editor or filter bar should show it filters for 'rule.level' >= 10 (or 10) and 'rule.groups' containing 'web'.

task_completion.description: Create a pie or donut chart for Attack Vectors
task_completion.visual_evidence: A pie or donut chart is visible. The legend, axis configuration, or tooltip should indicate it is aggregating by the field 'rule.description'.

task_completion.description: Create a horizontal bar chart for Top Attacker IPs
task_completion.visual_evidence: A horizontal bar chart is visible. The axis labels or configuration panel should show IP addresses, indicating aggregation by the field 'data.srcip'.

task_completion.description: Create a data table for Recent Web Incidents
task_completion.visual_evidence: A data table is visible. The column headers must include 'data.srcip' and 'data.url' (along with @timestamp, data.method, and rule.id).

task_completion.description: Save the dashboard containing all four visualizations
task_completion.visual_evidence: The final dashboard named 'Web_Attack_Analysis' is shown in view mode or a 'Saved' confirmation notification is visible, displaying all four required visualizations together.

integrity.description: The agent used the Wazuh/OpenSearch Dashboards web interface to create the dashboard
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the web browser and the Wazuh/OpenSearch Dashboards UI, rather than terminal commands or API scripts.

integrity.description: The visualizations are genuinely rendered in the UI
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show actual rendered charts, metrics, and tables with data, not just JSON payloads or text files.

integrity.description: The dashboard was not faked using browser developer tools
integrity.visual_evidence: The UI appears consistent with normal Wazuh Dashboards behavior, without signs of HTML manipulation or text editing to fake the titles or charts.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target dashboard must be named 'Web_Attack_Analysis' and include 4 visualizations. The evaluation script checks for specific configuration parameters rather than exact titles: a 'metric' type with filter_field 'rule.level' containing value '10', a 'pie' chart with agg_field 'rule.description', a 'horizontal_bar' with agg_field 'data.srcip', and a 'table' containing columns 'data.srcip' and 'data.url'. Exact visualization titles are ignored by the verifier, so focus on verifying the chart types and their data fields.
